title: DeepSeek Unveils New AI Models Narrowing Performance Gap
summary: DeepSeek has previewed two new AI models, DeepSeek V4 and DeepSeek V4+, that outperform its previous V3.2 model and significantly close the gap with leading models. The improvements come from architectural enhancements that boost efficiency and performance.
body:
DeepSeek has unveiled two new AI models, DeepSeek V4 and DeepSeek V4+, which the company claims are more efficient and performant than its previous DeepSeek V3.2 model. According to DeepSeek, these new models have almost closed the gap with current leading AI models, both open and closed, on reasoning benchmarks. The advancements are attributed to significant architectural improvements.

The announcement is notable as it positions DeepSeek as a strong competitor in the AI landscape, challenging both open-source and proprietary models. The efficiency gains suggest that DeepSeek is not just focusing on raw performance but also on making its models more accessible and cost-effective. This could have implications for industries relying on AI, from research to enterprise applications.

DeepSeek has not yet released the models publicly, but the preview indicates a significant leap forward. The company has hinted at future releases that could further narrow the performance gap. The AI community will be watching closely to see how these models perform in real-world applications and whether they can truly compete with the likes of GPT-5 and other frontier models.
twitterThread:
🚨 DeepSeek just dropped a bombshell: new AI models that *almost* close the gap with the big players. 

Here's what you need to know:

1. DeepSeek V4 and V4+ outperform V3.2 
2. Architectural improvements = big efficiency gains
3. Closing the gap with *both* open and closed models

The wild part? These models aren't just about raw power. They're more efficient too. 

That means lower costs, faster deployment, and broader accessibility. 

Let that sink in.

DeepSeek isn't just playing catch-up. They're positioning themselves as a serious contender. 

This changes everything for open-source AI. 

The gap is narrowing. Fast.

When will these models drop? DeepSeek hasn't said. But they're teasing future releases that could be even more disruptive. 

The AI arms race just got hotter.

Hot take: DeepSeek's move could force the big players to innovate faster. 

No more complacency. The game is on.
